Issue: interactive element too small to tap accurately
iOS Tool: Accessibility Inspector: tap target size audit; UIAccessibility.focusedElement
Android Tool: AccessibilityScanner app; Compose accessibilityTest; atLeast() matcher
Minimum Spec: iOS: 44×44pt minimum; Android: 48×48dp recommended (Google) 56×56dp for high-frequency
Do: Add transparent padding to extend tap area beyond visual bounds; use frame/padding modifiers
Don't: Make icon buttons 20×20 without padding; rely on tiny text links as only action
Code Example: iOS: .frame(minWidth:44,minHeight:44); Android: Modifier.minimumTouchTargetSize(48.dp)

Issue: element without accessible name label
iOS Tool: VoiceOver (iPhone) — swipe to navigate; enabled in Settings > Accessibility > VoiceOver
Android Tool: TalkBack (Android) — enabled in Settings > Accessibility > TalkBack
Minimum Spec: Every interactive element must have a name; images must have text alternative
Do: Add accessibilityLabel per element; describe function not appearance; images: brief description; decorative images: hidden
Don't: Icon buttons with no accessibilityLabel; labels that say 'Button' without context; duplicate labels for different elements
Code Example: iOS: .accessibilityLabel('Add to cart'); Android: contentDescription="Add to cart"

Issue: missing headings poor heading structure
iOS Tool: VoiceOver Rotor: Headings; navigate by heading
Android Tool: TalkBack heading navigation; AccessibilityNodeInfo.getExtras() heading check
Minimum Spec: H1 per screen; logical hierarchy H1 > H2 > H3; no skipped levels (H1 → H3)
Do: Mark screen title as H1; section headers as H2; avoid decorating text as heading for visual style only
Don't: Multiple H1s per screen; visual headings with no heading role; marking everything as heading for VoiceOver scrolling
Code Example: iOS: .accessibilityAddTraits(.header); Android: ViewCompat.setAccessibilityHeading(view, true)

Issue: focus order unexpected illogical tab order
iOS Tool: VoiceOver swiping order revealed in testing
Android Tool: TalkBack swiping order revealed; AccessibilityNodeInfoDumper
Minimum Spec: Focus must follow visual reading order (LTR: left→right top→bottom)
Do: Test by swiping through entire screen with VoiceOver/TalkBack; fix with accessibilitySortPriority; use semantic grouping
Don't: Focusable elements in DOM/view order that doesn't match visual layout; modal with focusable elements behind it reachable
Code Example: iOS: .accessibilitySortPriority(); Android: ViewCompat.setAccessibilityPaneTitle(); importantForAccessibility

Issue: dynamic type font scaling text size user preference
iOS Tool: VoiceOver with large text enabled (Settings > Accessibility > Larger Text); Simulator Accessibility > Font Scaling
Android Tool: TalkBack with Font Size increased; adb shell settings put system font_scale 1.3
Minimum Spec: iOS: must support Extra Small to Accessibility XXXLarge (up to 310% scale); Android: sp units scale automatically
Do: Use Dynamic Type text styles (title body caption); test at 200%+ scale; layouts must reflow not overflow
Don't: Hardcode font sizes in pt/px; clamp text to max 1 line at all sizes; define static width containers that can't expand
Code Example: iOS: Font.custom('Brand',size:17,relativeTo:.body); Android: textSize in sp units only

Issue: text contrast low insufficient foreground background
iOS Tool: Xcode Accessibility Inspector contrast checker; WWDC color audit tool
Android Tool: Material Color Utilities contrast checker; Compose Modifier.semantics; Color Contrast Analyzer desktop tool
Minimum Spec: Normal text: 4.5:1 minimum; Large text (18pt+ or 14pt bold+): 3:1 minimum; UI components: 3:1
Do: Test foreground text against all backgrounds including images; check in dark mode too; use contrast checker during design
Don't: Light gray on white; primary brand color text on white if insufficiently dark; placeholder gray as body text color
Code Example: Xcode Accessibility Inspector: View Hierarchy > Accessibility > contrast ratio value

Issue: color only information no other indicator
iOS Tool: Accessibility Inspector: display color blind simulation
Android Tool: AccessibilityScanner checks; phone has color blind simulation mode
Minimum Spec: Do not use color as the only way to convey information — add icon shape pattern or text
Do: Add icons or text alongside color coding (success=green+checkmark; error=red+X); use pattern in charts
Don't: Pure green/red traffic-light status with no other indicator; chart series differentiated only by color line only
Code Example: iOS: use SF Symbols .checkmark and .xmark alongside color; Android: use Warning / Check Material Icons with color

Issue: state change not announced dynamic content update
iOS Tool: VoiceOver: listen for automatic announcements; post UIAccessibility.post(notification:)
Android Tool: TalkBack: announceForAccessibility() or AccessibilityEvent.TYPE_ANNOUNCEMENT; Compose semantics liveRegion
Minimum Spec: All dynamic content changes must be communicated to assistive technology
Do: Post announcements for: async data loads errors form submission results search results count; use live regions
Don't: Silently swapping UI without announcement; posting announcements too frequently (notification storm)
Code Example: iOS: UIAccessibility.post(notification:.announcement, argument:'3 results found'); Android: ViewCompat.setAccessibilityLiveRegion(view, ViewCompat.ACCESSIBILITY_LIVE_REGION_POLITE)

Issue: swipe gesture custom action accessibility
iOS Tool: VoiceOver custom actions: swipe up through Actions rotor item
Android Tool: TalkBack: ViewCompat.addAccessibilityAction(); semantics block actions
Minimum Spec: Every gesture-only interaction needs an accessible alternative in 'Actions' menu
Do: Define UIAccessibilityCustomAction for every swipe/long-press/drag interaction; name actions clearly
Don't: Swipe-to-delete without Actions alternative; double-tap-hold gesture without custom action; drag reorder without up/down buttons
Code Example: iOS: accessibilityCustomActions=[UIAccessibilityCustomAction(name:'Delete',target:...)]; Compose: semantics { customActions = [...] }

Issue: element grouping accessibility focus clutter
iOS Tool: VoiceOver: each leaf being individually focused clutters navigation
Android Tool: TalkBack: too many focus stops per list row; screenReaderFocusable attribute
Minimum Spec: Group visually related elements into single accessible element where content is inseparable
Do: Use shouldGroupAccessibilityChildren / accessibilityElement(children:.combine) to group icon+label+value into one focus stop
Don't: Individually accessible label AND value AND button in same row when they're semantically one unit; user swipes 10× for simple info
Code Example: iOS: .accessibilityElement(children: .combine); .accessibilityLabel('Netflix, 4.5 stars, 2 hours'); Compose: semantics(mergeDescendants = true)

Issue: reduce motion animation accessibility preference
iOS Tool: Settings > Accessibility > Motion > Reduce Motion; UIAccessibility.isReduceMotionEnabled
Android Tool: Settings > Accessibility > Remove animations; AnimatorDurationScale adb shell setting
Minimum Spec: Disable or simplify animations when Reduce Motion is enabled
Do: Check UIAccessibility.isReduceMotionEnabled / reduceMotionEnabled before starting animations; provide fade instead of slide; test with ReduceMotion on
Don't: Full navigation slide transitions even with Reduce Motion on; background micro-animations that distract (reduced continuously)
Code Example: iOS: withAnimation(UIAccessibility.isReduceMotionEnabled ? .none : .spring()) {}; RN: AccessibilityInfo.isReduceMotionEnabled

Issue: image icon decorative meaningful alt text description
iOS Tool: Accessibility Inspector: identify images without labels; VoiceOver reads 'image' for unlabeled
Android Tool: TalkBack: reads file name or 'unlabeled' for images without contentDescription; Lint 'ImageContrastCheck'
Minimum Spec: Every informational image needs alt text; decorative images must be hidden from screen readers
Do: Write concise alt text (aim 0-150 chars); describe what the image conveys not what it looks like; hide decorative images
Don't: Alt text that repeats surrounding text; alt text 'image of...' (redundant); showing complex charts with no text alternative
Code Example: iOS: Image('logo').accessibilityLabel('Company Logo').accessibilityHidden(decorative: false); Compose: Image(contentDescription='Profile photo')

Issue: form input label error message field validation
iOS Tool: VoiceOver: test each input navigating and activating; announce label + hint + error
Android Tool: TalkBack: input fields must announce label; errorMessage via setError() announced automatically
Minimum Spec: Every input must have a persistent visible label; error messages must be associated with input; live announce on validation error
Do: Use placeholder AND label (placeholder disappears on type); associate error message with field; announce when error appears/clears
Don't: Placeholder-only inputs (no persistent label); error shown visually only without announcement; generic 'Invalid' error without context
Code Example: iOS: TextField('Name',text:$name).accessibilityHint('Enter your full name'); Android: TextInputLayout with setError()

Issue: keyboard navigation bluetooth keyboard external tablet
iOS Tool: Simulate bluetooth keyboard navigation in Accessibility Inspector; hardware keyboard in simulator
Android Tool: Test with physical keyboard connected to Android tablet; TabChain and focus visualization
Minimum Spec: All interactive elements must be reachable and operable via keyboard on tablet
Do: Support Tab/Shift+Tab navigation; Enter/Space activates; Escape/Back dismisses panels; arrow keys navigate lists
Don't: Elements that can only be activated by touch not keyboard; no visible focus ring; Tab order different from swipe/visual order
Code Example: iOS: keyboardShortcut(.tab, modifiers:[]); Compose: focusRequester(); HardwareBackHandler

Issue: error identification form field validation clear message
iOS Tool: VoiceOver: form error announcement; test submit with invalid data
Android Tool: TalkBack: setError() auto-announces; live region on error container
Minimum Spec: Error messages must describe what went wrong and how to fix it
Do: Error message: specific ('Password must be 8+ characters') not generic ('Invalid'); show in red AND with error icon symbol
Don't: 'Error!' with no context; error only shown via color change; no instructions on how to fix
Code Example: iOS: Text(errorMessage).foregroundColor(.red).accessibilityLabel(errorMessage); Android: TextInputLayout.setError('Password too short')

Issue: reduce transparency blur frosted glass vibrancy
iOS Tool: Settings > Accessibility > Display & Text > Reduce Transparency; UIAccessibility.isReduceTransparencyEnabled
Android Tool: Not applicable — Android has no system-level transparency reduction setting
Minimum Spec: Reduce blur/vibrancy when Reduce Transparency is enabled — replace with solid background versions
Do: Check isReduceTransparencyEnabled; use solid background color instead of UIBlurEffectStyle; test UI clarity without blur
Don't: All UI elements behind blur with no opaque fallback; navigation bar visible only through blur when disabled
Code Example: UIBlurEffect: if reduceTransparency backgroundColor=systemBackgroundColor else useBlur

Issue: voiceover rotor navigation headings links custom
iOS Tool: VoiceOver Rotor (rotate two fingers): Headings Links Words Characters Custom Items; test all rotor categories
Android Tool: TalkBack Local Context Menu equivalent; TalkBack headings navigation
Minimum Spec: App content must be navigable by all rotor categories especially Headings and Links
Do: Add heading traits to section headers; ensure links have descriptive text; add Custom Rotor for app-specific navigation (search filters)
Don't: Navigation that only works by linear swipe; app with no headings making Headings rotor useless; custom rotor items with generic names
Code Example: UIAccessibilityCustomRotor(name:'Tasks') { info in ... }; accessibilityAddTraits(.header)

Issue: talkback explore touch finger feedback
iOS Tool: Not applicable — iOS uses different model
Android Tool: TalkBack Explore by Touch: hover finger over screen to hear items under finger; test all interactive elements are reachable
Minimum Spec: Every interactive element must be reachable by dragging finger around screen
Do: Test by enabling TalkBack and dragging finger across all interactive areas; ensure overlapping views don't block
Don't: Interactive elements covered by decorative overlay; custom touch regions that don't receive hover events in TalkBack
Code Example: android:importantForAccessibility='yes'; Modifier.semantics { contentDescription = ... }; avoid clickable on containers

Issue: rich content data table chart graph accessible
iOS Tool: VoiceOver: tables navigated by row/column; charts announced as image by default; custom rotor needed
Android Tool: TalkBack: tables navigated by grid; charts need contentDescription or custom implementation
Minimum Spec: Charts and complex data must have accessible text alternative or accessible data table
Do: For charts: provide 'View as Table' button; for data tables: use proper table markup with headers; announce total trend in chart description
Don't: Image-only chart with no text alternative; data table with no row/column header association; interactive chart with no keyboard/accessible controls
Code Example: iOS: List with ForEach for accessible table; chart + Button('View data as list'); Android: RecyclerView with table role

Issue: reading order dom view order visual
iOS Tool: VoiceOver: swipe and confirm element reads in expected visual order
Android Tool: TalkBack: linearization audit; visualize focus via Developer Options > Show layout bounds check order
Minimum Spec: Visual reading order (top-to-bottom LTR) must match swipe/focus order
Do: Use semantic structure to control reading order; use accessibilitySortPriority to override; test flow with VoiceOver swipe
Don't: Absolutely positioned elements read in insertion order not visual order; content in wrong z-order reads out of sequence
Code Example: iOS: .accessibilitySortPriority(100) for priority; Android: android:accessibilityTraversalBefore/After

Issue: touch target physical 1cm minimum nngroup research study
iOS Tool: Accessibility Inspector; measure on physical device with ruler — digital specs (pt/dp) don't communicate physical reality
Android Tool: AccessibilityScanner; test on actual device not emulator
Minimum Spec: 1cm × 1cm physical minimum (NNGroup / Parhi et al.); MIT Touch Lab: avg fingertip 1.6-2cm; avg thumb 2.5cm impact area; 44pt on 2x = ~7mm (BELOW 1cm — minimum is a compromise)
Do: For apps used in motion (in-store walking driving): increase targets to 2cm×2cm; primary CTAs 2/3+ screen width; test on physical device not simulator where clicks are pixel-perfect
Don't: Designing touch targets that pass 44pt spec in Xcode but feel impossible on actual device; assuming simulator click accuracy equals real finger accuracy
Code Example: Measure: 44pt on @3x = 44×3=132px at 3x ≈ 11mm ✓; 32pt on @3x = 32×3=96px ≈ 8mm ✗ below 1cm

Issue: view tap asymmetry visible small tappable impossible
iOS Tool: Test: can you accurately tap the element without hitting adjacent elements? Measure physical size
Android Tool: AccessibilityScanner; TalkBack Explore by Touch: dragging finger must land accurately on correct target
Minimum Spec: Elements that are readable/visible to eye but untappable accurately by finger — e.g. carousel dots (1-2mm), color swatches, pagination indicators
Do: If an element is interactive it must pass 1cm physical tap target minimum — not just visible size; add invisible tap area extension if visual size must stay small
Don't: Carousel dot indicators as primary navigation without prev/next buttons; 6px × 6px pagination dots; tiny color swatches on product grids
Code Example: iOS: ZStack { Dot().frame(8).padding(18) } // 8pt visual 44pt tappable; Android: View 8dp visual touchDelegate 48dp tappable

Issue: touch target gap spacing adjacent crowding slip error
iOS Tool: Test rapid sequential taps on adjacent targets; observe error rate on physical device
Android Tool: AccessibilityScanner spacing check; manual test of adjacent action buttons
Minimum Spec: Minimum 8dp gap between adjacent interactive elements; elements that are same size but adjacent can still cause mistaps if gap <8dp
Do: Space stacked action buttons 8-16dp apart; place destructive actions (Delete) far from safe actions (Save); for same-density layouts use single-target rows
Don't: Stacked buttons with 2dp gap (NNGroup Instagram dismiss failure study); Delete at same spacing as Edit in list row; icon buttons horizontally packed without gap
Code Example: Minimum 8pt/dp between any two touchable regions; for error-sensitive pairs (delete/confirm) use 24pt+ separation

Issue: coach mark tip overlay single focused one at a time sequential
iOS Tool: VoiceOver: announce when coach mark appears; is it focus-trapped? Can user dismiss with accessible action?
Android Tool: TalkBack: focus must move to coach mark tooltip on appearance; dismiss via back/swipe
Minimum Spec: Coach marks must be single-focused: one tip at a time; max 5 in entire onboarding sequence; tips must be dismissible and never block critical UI permanently
Do: Show one coach mark at a time contextually; include visual arrow pointing to feature; use ultra-short copy <15 words; present at moment of first relevant action
Don't: Series of 8+ coach marks on first launch; tips that auto-dismiss in 1.5s leaving screen reader no time to read; coach mark shown every session
Code Example: Announce appearance via live region; trap focus; provide custom accessibility action 'Dismiss hint'
